title: Exec memo on org design trade-offs
description: Real-world PM scenario on exec memo on org design trade-offs with conflicting stakeholder incentives and incomplete data.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at OpsForge, a Series C internal tools company (430 employees, $49.1M ARR). Your immediate assignment is exec memo on org design trade-offs. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Make trade-offs explicit so every team understands what we are not doing.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 38411
New logo adds: 3872
Activation rate: 20%
30-day retention: 34%
NPS: 36 (n=41)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 30%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 1 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 2 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Evan Carter): "If we cannot show credible progress this quarter, procurement pushes these deals into next fiscal."
VP Engineering (Mina Cole): "Scope drift, not coding speed, is the main reason timelines slip. Lock boundaries now."
Head of Customer Success (Evan Choi): "High-noise accounts dominate feedback threads, but silent churn in mid-market is the bigger risk."
Commercial note: 4 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 35 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Helio Health Group (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Brightlane Education (IT Manager): "We needed clearer ownership between implementation and support during rollout."
Orbit Travel Co (Director of Product): "I would expand if reporting and error states were easier for non-technical users."
Data gap: no structured interviews from churned accounts in the last 30 days.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 48
Median cycle time: 27 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 51%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 4
Release rollback events last quarter: 4
Average time to resolve escalations: 25 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does it handle disagreement constructively and concretely?

rubric.name: Credibility
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 20% and retention is 34%, while NPS (36, n=41) and 35 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Evan Carter around the 4 deal pipeline, technical caution from Mina Cole on dependency risk (4 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Evan Choi due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 38411 active accounts, activation 20%, retention 34%, and on-time delivery 51%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
